---
title: Tags
description: Group cache entries and invalidate them together with invalidateTag.
icon: Tag
---

## What are tags?

Tags let you associate one or more logical labels with a cached entry. When you call `invalidateTag`, all entries carrying that tag are deleted — regardless of their individual keys.

**Use tags when** you need to:
- Invalidate all cached data related to an entity after a mutation
- Clear a group of keys without knowing them individually

## Assign tags

Pass `tags` as an array in `set` or `wrap` options:

```ts
await cache.set("post:1", post, {
  ttl: "10m",
  tags: ["post:1", "user:42:posts"],
});

const post = await cache.wrap(
  "post:1",
  () => db.posts.findById(1),
  { ttl: "10m", tags: ["post:1", "user:42:posts"] }
);
```

## Invalidate by tag

```ts
// After updating a post
await cache.invalidateTag("post:1");

// After a user's posts change
await cache.invalidateTag("user:42:posts");
```

## Invalidate multiple tags

```ts
await cache.invalidateTags(["post:1", "user:42:posts"]);
```

## Naming conventions

Tags are arbitrary strings — choose a convention that makes sense for your domain:

| Pattern | Example |
|---------|---------|
| Entity + ID | `"user:42"`, `"post:1"` |
| Entity collection | `"users"`, `"posts"` |
| Owner's collection | `"user:42:posts"` |
| Feature group | `"dashboard:metrics"` |

## Adapter support

Not all adapters support tag-based invalidation natively. The core emulates it on adapters that do not expose `invalidateTag` by maintaining an internal reverse-index of `tag → [key]`.

| Adapter | Native tag support |
|---------|-------------------|
| Memory | Emulated |
| Redis | Emulated |
| Upstash | Emulated |
| Cloudflare KV | Emulated |
| Vercel KV | Emulated |

**Note**: On adapters without native support, the reverse-index is stored in the same backend. For large-scale tag invalidation, prefer Redis or Upstash with a custom adapter that uses Redis sets.
